sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation['nodes']
after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation['nodes']
before_by_id = {n['id']: n for n in before}
after_by_id = {n['id']: n for n in after}

for nid, n in after_by_id.items():
    if nid in before_by_id:
        bn = before_by_id[nid]
        if n['pixels'] != bn['pixels'] or n['boundary'] != bn['boundary']:
            print(f"Node {nid}: color={n['color']}, pixels {bn['pixels']}->{n['pixels']}, boundary {bn['boundary']}->{n['boundary']}")

# Check the green container (node 2) and yellow bar (node 16)
for n in after:
    if n['id'] in [2, 16, 4, 17]:
        print(f"Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Very interesting! Clicking on the second light green shape (node 14) caused the green container (node 2) to grow back from 5 to 14 pixels! It's the reverse of the first click. So:
- Click on first N shape (node 13): green container shrinks (14→5)
- Click on second N shape (node 14): green container grows back (5→14)
This means the two shapes in each container have opposite effects! One shrinks and one grows the matching colored object. The goal is probably to shrink all the colored objects completely.
So the mechanic is:
- Each container has two shapes that control a matching-colored object elsewhere
- One shape shrinks it, the other grows it
- The goal is to shrink all colored objects to nothing
Now I need to figure out which shape in each container is the "shrink" one and which is the "grow" one. For the light green shapes:
- Node 13 (left compartment, at ~21,39): shrinks
- Node 14 (right compartment, at ~21,45): grows
Now let me check the yellow shapes in the bottom container (node 18). I need to figure out which one shrinks and which one grows the yellow bar (node 16).
Let me click on one of the yellow shapes to test.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The two N shapes have opposite effects: left shrinks, right grows.
# Now test the Y shapes in the bottom container.
# Node 20 (top Y shape): boundary [[37, 23], [37, 25], [39, 25], [38, 25], [37, 24], [38, 23], [39, 23]]
# Node 23 (bottom Y shape): boundary [[43, 23], [44, 23], [45, 24], [44, 25], [43, 25], [45, 25], [45, 23]]
# Try clicking the top Y shape first (node 20, center ~38,24)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 38, 'col': 24}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
